title: ipomoea funis var. langlassei
intro: Ipomoea funis var. langlassei, commonly known as Langlasse's morning glory, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Langlasse's morning glory include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and therapeutic applications of Ipomoea funis var. langlassei.
constituents: Ipomoea funis var. langlassei has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds, including ipomoeoside, langlassein, and funisine, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, potentially improving cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of certain cancers. The flavonoids present in Ipomoea funis var. langlassei, such as quercetin and kaempferol, may help to enhance cognitive function and protect against neurodegenerative diseases, while the glycosides can aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. By harnessing the medicinal potential of these constituents, individuals can experience improved overall well-being and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
side_effects: Ipomoea funis var. langlassei can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea and vomiting, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds like ergine and lysergic acid amide. Ingestion of large quantities can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and confusion, as the plant's psychoactive properties can cause altered mental states. Additionally, improper use of Ipomoea funis var. langlassei can trigger allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory issues, in susceptible individuals. In severe cases, excessive consumption of the plant can cause hallucinations, seizures, and even death, emphasizing the importance of caution and proper guidance when handling this species.
